text.tspan: AUDIT LOG: an immutable, append-only record of
security-/compliance-relevant events.
Distinct from application logs and access logs.

g.text: WHAT GOES IN AN AUDIT LOG

text.tspan: NOT IN AUDIT LOG
u2022 routine application log lines (those go to app logs)
u2022 routine HTTP access logs (those go to access logs)
u2022 secrets themselves u2014 log that a secret was rotated, NOT its value
u2022 raw PII when not needed for audit u2014 hash/redact instead
MUST-HAVE PROPERTIES
u2022 IMMUTABLE: append-only, no edits, no deletes
u2022 TAMPER-EVIDENT: hashes / digital signatures / WORM storage
u2022 LONG-RETAINED: years (regulatory: SOX, HIPAA, PCI-DSS)
u2022 SEPARATELY-STORED: not in the system being audited
u2022 ACL-PROTECTED: read-restricted; write-only for the auditing service
u2022 TIMELY: written reliably even under load (often via outbox)
u2022 QUERYABLE: investigators need to search by user, time, entity
u2022 COMPLETE: no gaps in sequence (gap = suspicious)
ENTITY CHANGES (CRUD on sensitive data)
u2022 create / update / delete of customer records, accounts, permissions
u2022 changes to billing data, configuration, secrets
AUTH EVENTS
u2022 login success / failure, MFA, password reset
u2022 role assignment changes, permission grants/revokes
PRIVILEGED ACTIONS
u2022 admin views customer data; support impersonates user
u2022 access to PHI / PII / payment card data
SYSTEM EVENTS
u2022 schema migration applied; secret rotated; deployment
u2022 export of bulk data
RECORD FIELDS
who (user / service principal / API key)
what (action + entity + before/after state)
when (server-side timestamp, UTC)
where (source IP, user-agent, region)
why (business reason / ticket reference if available)
correlation_id
